scope: Next 10 incomplete transfer rows after transfer-research-001
method: Research player role/minutes expectation from roster pages, school announcements, transfer reports, projected lineups, and team context.
Use historical rating from the imported spreadsheet as the historical anchor.
Translate evidence into the manual input fields used by the spreadsheet: starter, role, opportunity change, offensive burden, NBA projection, and upside/tools.
Cap starter/role/opportunity when projected-lineup or depth-chart evidence is unclear.
Keep every researched suggestion in review status until manually accepted.

teamContextRubric.projectedStarter: Use Yes only with projected-lineup evidence, explicit likely-starter reporting, or a roster context with a clear open starting path. Use Uncertain for high-minute players without clear starting evidence.
teamContextRubric.projectedRole: Cap likely rotation or specialist profiles in the 60-75 range. Use 80-89 for likely starters or top-four options. Use 90+ only for projected primary/near-primary stars.
teamContextRubric.opportunityChange: Do not reward prior production by itself. Lower-major stars moving into crowded high-major rosters should usually sit in the 45-65 range unless reporting shows a clear role increase.
teamContextRubric.offensiveBurden: Tie burden to expected usage within the new lineup, not just prior usage.
teamContextRubric.roleCap: Each researched player gets a role cap based on projected lineup, returning stars, incoming stars, position competition, and starter evidence.

players.researchSummary: Strong starter/lead-guard signal. CBS projects him in Boston College's starting lineup, BC coverage frames him as a main offensive focal point, and Boston College underwent major roster turnover under a new coach. The high-major jump keeps him below true 90+ star treatment, but opportunity is legitimately strong.

teamContext.projectedLineup: CBS projects him as a starting guard for Boston College.
teamContext.returningStars: BC had heavy roster turnover, reducing incumbency pressure.
teamContext.incomingStars: BC added many portal pieces, but Williams is the most proven scorer in the group.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Other guards are present, but his scoring/playmaking profile projects near the top of the backcourt.
teamContext.starterEvidence: direct projected-lineup evidence plus roster-reset context
teamContext.roleCap: likely starter/lead scorer; cap role high-80s until ACC translation is proven

evidence.notes: Projected Boston College starting lineup includes Money Williams.
Reported BC commitment; 20.6 ppg, 4.3 rpg, 4.7 apg; Big Sky scoring leader.
Describes Williams as one of the main focal points for BC's offense.
Confirms 2026-27 BC roster placement.

players.researchSummary: Good starter signal. CBS projects him as Stanford's starting guard, and Stanford's release says he can step in and help immediately after a massive Delaware role. The role is capped low-80s because Stanford also has a strong freshman/newcomer group and Bliss is moving up from CUSA/Delaware to the ACC.

teamContext.projectedLineup: CBS projects him as a starting guard.
teamContext.returningStars: Stanford lost important guard production, creating guard opportunity.
teamContext.incomingStars: Stanford has talented freshmen/newcomers expected to get real minutes.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Perimeter depth exists, but his production/playmaking gives him a starting path.
teamContext.roleCap: likely starter/major creator; cap role low-80s due to ACC jump and newcomer competition

evidence.notes: Stanford signing release; CUSA Freshman of the Year; 16.7 ppg, 5.2 rpg, 5.9 apg, 39.8 mpg; coach praises versatility.
Projected Stanford starting lineup includes Christian Bliss.

players.researchSummary: Strong experience and production, but starter status should stay uncertain because USF added a full 15-man roster with multiple guards and four-star talent. Wilson's 98 starts and 17.0/4.6 profile support major minutes, but the team-context cap keeps role under 80 until a projected lineup confirms him.

teamContext.returningStars: USF has no returning players from the 2025-26 squad.
teamContext.incomingStars: USF added 15 players, including 12 transfers and multiple four-star recruits.
teamContext.positionCompetition: crowded guard group includes Mike James, Chris Davis Jr., AJ Dancler and others
teamContext.starterEvidence: production and experience support possible starter, but no projected-lineup source found
teamContext.roleCap: major rotation/possible starter; cap role high-70s pending lineup

evidence.notes: Confirms Sonny Wilson on 2026-27 roster; roster includes several guards and transfers.
98 career starts at Toledo; 1,338 points; 17.0 ppg and 4.6 apg as junior.
USF added 15 players, 12 via portal, with multiple highly rated additions.

players.researchSummary: Versatile, productive forward stepping up to Cincinnati. His Towson production is strong and Cincinnati's release praises positional versatility, but no projected-lineup source was found and Cincinnati's 2025-26 roster context includes multiple frontcourt/wing pieces. Cap as strong rotation/possible starter.

teamContext.returningStars: Cincinnati has returning frontcourt/wing context that needs a separate projected-lineup pass.
teamContext.incomingStars: Tejada is a significant portal addition but not clearly identified as a starter.
teamContext.positionCompetition: frontcourt/wing role competition likely at Big 12 level
teamContext.starterEvidence: coach quote highlights versatility, not an explicit starter role
teamContext.roleCap: strong rotation/possible starter; cap role mid-70s

evidence.notes: 2026-27 roster bio; prior Towson production, CAA honors, 17.7 ppg and 5.5 rpg.
Cincinnati signing announcement; coach says Tejada can play multiple positions.
All-CAA Third Team; preseason CAA Player of the Year; 1,000-point scorer.

players.researchSummary: This looks more like a returning-star profile than a normal transfer. Cal Poly's roster confirms him back, and 2025-26 data shows he led the Big West in scoring while starting 30 games. Role and burden are elite; opportunity change is only moderate because he is staying in the same team context rather than gaining a new role.

teamContext.projectedLineup: returning Cal Poly star; starter status strongly supported by prior starts and roster return
teamContext.returningStars: Cayden Ward and other Cal Poly contributors return, but Mousa is the clear scoring anchor
teamContext.incomingStars: no incoming player found that displaces his role
teamContext.positionCompetition: role is secure unless roster changes substantially
teamContext.starterEvidence: 2026-27 roster confirms return; 30 starts and league-leading scoring in 2025-26
teamContext.roleCap: primary returning star; role can reach mid-90s, but opportunity change should not be high because context is stable

evidence.notes: Confirms 2026-27 Cal Poly roster placement.
2025-26 Cal Poly: 30 starts, 31.3 mpg, 20.4 ppg, 6.3 rpg, 37.2% 3FG.
Big West scoring leader by overall stats.

players.researchSummary: Strong role and starter signal. Indiana's roster confirms him, Indiana coverage highlights him as a major part of the roster rebuild, and SI projects him as the starting power forward. Burden stays below lead-creator territory because Indiana also has Markus Burton, Samet Yigitoglu, Jaeden Mustaf, and other pieces.

teamContext.projectedLineup: SI projects him as starting power forward.
teamContext.returningStars: Indiana has multiple major pieces around him, including guard and center talent.
teamContext.incomingStars: transfer class is highly ranked and deep.
teamContext.positionCompetition: frontcourt has size, but Sherrell is projected as premier interior presence.
teamContext.roleCap: likely starter/high-impact frontcourt piece; cap role high-80s because he is not necessarily primary offensive engine

evidence.notes: Confirms Sherrell on 2026-27 roster.
Indiana transfer class ranked highly; article highlights Sherrell as part of roster rebuild.
Projects Sherrell as starting power forward and premier interior presence.

players.researchSummary: Elite shooter and obvious Baylor need-fit, but role should be capped as specialist/rotation until a projected lineup emerges. Baylor signed multiple transfers and has guard competition, so prior Liberty scoring does not automatically become a starting Big 12 role.

teamContext.returningStars: Baylor guard/wing room needs full lineup pass.
teamContext.incomingStars: Baylor signed several transfer additions after Decker.
teamContext.positionCompetition: shooting need creates path, but there is meaningful guard competition
teamContext.starterEvidence: Baylor need-fit, no direct starter projection
teamContext.roleCap: specialist/rotation shooter; cap role around 70 until lineup supports more

evidence.notes: Baylor signing release; 16.9 ppg and 47% from three at Liberty.
Describes him as one of the nation's top returning three-point shooters.
Baylor needed guard shooting; Decker viewed as portal answer to spacing/shooting need.
2026-27 roster includes Decker and multiple other guards/transfers.

players.researchSummary: High-upside young guard with strong Kansas coach endorsement. Bill Self called him one of the best guard prospects in the portal and said he can be lead/scoring guard, but Kansas also has major incoming backcourt talent. Treat as high-minute possible starter, not automatic lead guard.

teamContext.returningStars: Kansas roster includes other high-end recruits/transfers.
teamContext.incomingStars: Kansas fall signees include multiple guards; portal class also adds talent.
teamContext.positionCompetition: guard competition likely high at Kansas
teamContext.starterEvidence: coach praise is strong, but no direct projected lineup
teamContext.roleCap: high-upside possible starter/major rotation; cap role around 80

evidence.notes: Bill Self quote calls him one of the best guard prospects in the portal; MAC Freshman of the Year; 16.4 ppg, 4.5 apg, 40.7% 3FG.
Commitment report; 32 starts, 16.4 ppg, 4.0 rpg, 4.5 apg.
High portal rating and Kansas commitment.

players.researchSummary: Do not treat as normal transfer until eligibility is settled. He is reportedly returning to Florida but needs a fifth-year waiver. If eligible, his Kentucky production suggests a useful veteran rotation guard, but Florida's strong roster context and waiver uncertainty cap role/opportunity sharply.

teamContext.returningStars: Florida projects as a strong roster with multiple key pieces.
teamContext.incomingStars: Florida roster strength reduces path to high burden.
teamContext.positionCompetition: veteran guard minutes possible if eligible, but not starter lock
teamContext.roleCap: eligibility hold; cap role mid-60s until waiver and lineup context clear

evidence.notes: Reports Aberdeen returning to Florida and needing NCAA waiver for fifth year.
Background on Aberdeen as winning guard, former Florida sixth man, three-level scorer/defender.

players.researchSummary: Strong scorer and important early portal addition for a new NC State staff, but not an automatic starter because NC State added a deep transfer group with multiple guards. His Santa Clara production, First-Team All-WCC profile, and NBA-fit comments support meaningful role and upside.

teamContext.returningStars: new staff and transfer-heavy roster creates uncertainty
teamContext.incomingStars: NC State added multiple transfers, including Preston Edmead and Darius Adams in guard spots
teamContext.positionCompetition: high-volume guard competition; Hammond has strong claim but not a lock
teamContext.starterEvidence: first transfer of new staff and strong production; no direct projected-lineup source
teamContext.roleCap: major rotation/possible starter; cap role high-70s

evidence.notes: First-Team All-WCC; started 34 of 35 games; led Santa Clara with 15.6 ppg.
Hammond says NC State puts him in best position to chase NBA goal; left major void at Santa Clara.
Shows NC State's transfer-heavy guard roster context.
Lists NC State arrivals and stats, including multiple guard additions.
